metadata.name: quickjs
metadata.description: QuickJS compiler. Compiles JS source — last expression must be a callable — into a runtime Fn via QuickJS-emscripten. JS runs inside a wasm sandbox: no DOM, no eval, no host APIs unless explicitly granted via the host segment. Sub stitutes for Javy (same QuickJS interpreter, npm-native distribution). Lazy-loads the runtime on first compile.
Bootstrap switch. Lazy: the QuickJS runtime (~1MB) loads on first quickjs cel compile. Defaults true.
Runtime-ready sentinel. Stays true at boot (lazy-load masks the transition); v2 with explicit lifecycle will flip false→true at runtime spawn.
Catastrophic-death sentinel. Main-thread QuickJS doesn't crash without taking the process down; trivially true. Becomes meaningful with worker isolation.
Append-only log of CelError values from quickjs lambdas. v1: empty — trap-as-value stores errors on the failing cel's v.

metadata.name: quickjs-to-js
metadata.description: Bridge: convert a quickjs-domain value to a JS-domain value. v1 wrapper Fns already vm.dump() handles into native JS, so this is identity. Becomes a real marshalling call when composite handles + worker isolation arrive.

metadata.name: js-to-quickjs
metadata.description: Bridge: convert a JS-domain value to a quickjs-domain value. Identity at v1 — the wrapper Fn marshals JS args into VM handles automatically at call boundary.
